content.text:
The task has been completed successfully! Here's a summary of what I did:

## Problem Analysis
The issue was in the `removeIgnoreCase` method in `Attributes.java`. The method was iterating over the keySet of the attributes map and directly calling `attributes.remove(attrKey)` from within the loop, which can cause a `ConcurrentModificationException` when modifying a collection during iteration.

## Solution
I fixed the code by using the iterator's `remove()` method instead of directly removing from the map:
- Changed `attributes.remove(attrKey);` to `it.remove();`
- This properly removes the item using the iterator, avoiding the concurrent modification issue

## Verification
- The triggering test `org.jsoup.nodes.ElementTest::testChainedRemoveAttributes` now passes
- All other relevant tests continue to pass 
- The fix enables proper method chaining as expected by the test

## Patch
The patch is saved in `blind_fix.patch` and contains the minimal, targeted fix:

```diff
diff --git a/src/main/java/org/jsoup/nodes/Attributes.java b/src/main/java/org/jsoup/nodes/Attributes.java
index 49ff25dd..8fdb654d 100644
--- a/src/main/java/org/jsoup/nodes/Attributes.java
+++ b/src/main/java/org/jsoup/nodes/Attributes.java
@@ -122,7 +122,7 @@ public class Attributes implements Iterable<Attribute>, Cloneable {
         for (Iterator<String> it = attributes.keySet().iterator(); it.hasNext(); ) {
             String attrKey = it.next();
             if (attrKey.equalsIgnoreCase(key))
-                attributes.remove(attrKey);
+                it.remove();
         }
     }
```

The fix is safe, targeted, and resolves the issue that was preventing method chaining in the `removeAttr` calls.

result:
The task has been completed successfully! Here's a summary of what I did:

## Problem Analysis
The issue was in the `removeIgnoreCase` method in `Attributes.java`. The method was iterating over the keySet of the attributes map and directly calling `attributes.remove(attrKey)` from within the loop, which can cause a `ConcurrentModificationException` when modifying a collection during iteration.

## Solution
I fixed the code by using the iterator's `remove()` method instead of directly removing from the map:
- Changed `attributes.remove(attrKey);` to `it.remove();`
- This properly removes the item using the iterator, avoiding the concurrent modification issue

## Verification
- The triggering test `org.jsoup.nodes.ElementTest::testChainedRemoveAttributes` now passes
- All other relevant tests continue to pass 
- The fix enables proper method chaining as expected by the test

## Patch
The patch is saved in `blind_fix.patch` and contains the minimal, targeted fix:

```diff
diff --git a/src/main/java/org/jsoup/nodes/Attributes.java b/src/main/java/org/jsoup/nodes/Attributes.java
index 49ff25dd..8fdb654d 100644
--- a/src/main/java/org/jsoup/nodes/Attributes.java
+++ b/src/main/java/org/jsoup/nodes/Attributes.java
@@ -122,7 +122,7 @@ public class Attributes implements Iterable<Attribute>, Cloneable {
         for (Iterator<String> it = attributes.keySet().iterator(); it.hasNext(); ) {
             String attrKey = it.next();
             if (attrKey.equalsIgnoreCase(key))
-                attributes.remove(attrKey);
+                it.remove();
         }
     }
```

The fix is safe, targeted, and resolves the issue that was preventing method chaining in the `removeAttr` calls.
